summary: Build a food website called Farm2Retail.

existing_features.title: Create the Farm2Retail website shell with a responsive layout (header, main content, footer) and consistent navigation. The header must display the site name “Farm2Retail” and include nav links to Home, Products, Farms, About, and Contact.

Implement a coherent visual theme suitable for a farm-to-retail food brand and apply it consistently across all pages (colors, typography, spacing, button styles, cards). Avoid blue/purple as primary brand colors.

Build the Products page that loads products from the backend and supports client-side search and filtering (at minimum: search by name and filter by category). Display products in a responsive card grid showing image, name, price/unit, category, and stock status.
Add a Product Details page that displays full product information (including description) and the associated farm summary, and provides a link to the Farm Details page for that farm.
Build the Farms page that lists farms from the backend and a Farm Details page that shows the farm profile and a list of products from that farm (derived by filtering the product list by farmId).

Create Home, About, and Contact pages with English user-facing copy appropriate for “Farm2Retail” (farm-to-retail marketplace concept). The Home page should include a hero section and clear calls to action linking to Products and Farms.

Add required static image assets to the frontend and use them in the UI (logo and hero/banner, plus a small set of product/farm placeholders). Store generated images under frontend/public/assets/generated and reference them directly from the frontend.

Add a Cart option (icon button) to the site header, positioned on the top-right side and visible from all pages. The Cart option must be accessible (screen-reader label) and must not break the existing responsive header behavior (desktop nav + mobile sheet menu).
Create a basic Cart page and route so that clicking the Cart option navigates to a dedicated cart screen (e.g., "/cart") with English user-facing text and an empty-state message.

new_feature_requests.summary: Create a food website named Farm2Retail.

Add a backend data model and APIs to support browsing content for Farm2Retail: farms and products. Each product should include at minimum: id, name, category, price, unit, description, image URL (string), farmId, inStock (bool). Each farm should include at minimum: id, name, location, description, image URL (string). Provide query methods to list products, list farms, and fetch product/farm by id.
Seed the backend with a small set of example farms and products so the UI is populated on first load without requiring manual data entry.
Add a Cart option/icon in the site header (top-right) accessible from all pages.
